{{Shrine |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (qq23) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (qq23). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds qq6 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (qq23) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (qq23). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds qq6 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (qq23) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (qq23). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds qq6 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Ōkusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * revision.comment: Text replacement - "Kamō no Ōkusu" to "Kamō no Kusu"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == *
