{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Special Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]]

During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|12=draft|12=draft}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘|12=draft|12=draft}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|12=draft|12=draft}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Special Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[Category:New]]

It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Special Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai

It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Special Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]]

revision.comment: 43 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市) up to rev 101069439
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 蒲生八幡神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |所在地 = 鹿児島県姶良市蒲生町上久徳2259-1 |位置 = |ISO=JP-46 | 緯度度 = 31 |緯度分 = 45 |緯度秒 = 57.2 | 経度度 = 130 |経度分 = 34 |経度秒 =10.9 |祭神 = 応神天皇
仲哀天皇
神功皇后 |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = 保安4年（1123年） |本殿 = [[神明造]] |別名 = 正八幡若宮八幡宮（旧名） |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月19日 |神事 = |地図 = Japan Kagoshima }} '''蒲生八幡神社'''（かもうはちまんじんじゃ）は[[鹿児島県]][[姶良市]][[蒲生町 (鹿児島県)|蒲生町]][[上久徳]]にある[[神社]]。旧名は「'''正八幡若宮八幡宮'''」。 == 祭神 == [[応神天皇]]、[[仲哀天皇]]、[[神功皇后]]の[[八幡三神]]を祀る。 == 摂社・末社 == * 四所宮（[[仁徳天皇]]・[[菟道稚郎子|宇治皇子]]・ウレ姫・クレ姫） * 武内神社（[[武内宿禰]]） * 天社（[[天神七代]]） * 國社（[[地神五代]]） * 早風社（[[大人隼人]]） * 十八種神社 == 歴史 == [[藤原北家|藤原氏北家]][[藤原北家#教通流|教通流]]の分出とされる[[蒲生氏#大隅蒲生氏|蒲生（かもう）氏]]の初代当主[[蒲生舜清|舜清（ちかきよ）]]が[[保安 (元号)|保安]]4年（[[1123年]]）に[[大隅国]]へ下向、生母が[[宇佐八幡宮]]の宮司家出身であった縁で同宮から[[勧請]]して創祀したという。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]、[[島津氏]]との激しい戦いの末に蒲生氏は敗れて島津氏の軍門に下り、[[弘治 (日本)|弘治]]3年（[[1557年]]）他地に退去させられた。その後[[島津義弘]]により社殿が再建される。また[[元和 (日本)|元和]]4年（[[1618年]]）12月には義弘から[[鳥居]]と額が奉建されたともされる。 [[昭和]]60年（[[1985年]]）、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の被害により社殿は大破し、現在の社殿はその後再建したものである。また、台風被害の翌年から「蒲生八幡神社」と改名した。2008年頃から社務所を鳥居の近くより社殿左に移転している。 == 文化財 == [[ファイル:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|180px|蒲生の大クス]] === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 銅鏡 秋草双雀文様 1面 - 直径20センチ強の[[鎌倉時代]]の[[銅鏡]]。鏡背に秋草と2羽の[[雀]]を鋳出し、当時の進んだ工芸技術を示す[http://www.town.aira-aira.kagoshima.jp/kamou/main-f/rekishitanbo/furusato-f/furusato2.htm 蒲生町役場、「銅鏡 秋草双雀文様一面」（ふるさとの歴史をたずねて其ノ弐）]（平成22年10月2日閲覧）。。[[大正]]7年（[[1918年]]）4月8日に当時の[[古社寺保存法]]に基づき国宝（旧国宝、1950年の[[文化財保護法]]施行後は国の[[重要文化財]]）に指定。なおこの他にも116面の銅鏡が所蔵され、当神社は鹿児島県下で最も多くの銅鏡を所蔵する神社となっている。 === 特別天然記念物 === * [[蒲生のクス]] - 推定樹齢1,600年（平成22年現在）の[[楠]]の大木で。1952年（昭和27年）、国の[[特別天然記念物]]に指定された。1988年（昭和63年）度の[[環境庁]]による巨樹・巨木林調査で日本一の巨樹であると認定された。[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件]]で大隅へ配流された[[和気清麻呂]]が当地を訪れ、手にしていた杖を地に刺したものが根付いたものと伝えられ、神社創祀時には既に[[神木]]として祀られていたという[http://powerdesign.bbplus.net/0_Kamou80000/KamouSite/oukusu/oukusu.html 蒲生八幡神社、「日本一の巨木」]（平成22年10月2日閲覧）。。 == その他 == * 額の裏には社殿再建に関わった人物の名前が記載されていた。 * 社殿のすぐ近くには[[姶良市立蒲生小学校|蒲生小学校]]が建っているが、かつて寺院があったとされ、昭和初期に墓地移転の際に地中から巨大な仏像が発掘された。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html 蒲生八幡神社]（公式ホームページ） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもうはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:鹿児島県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:鹿児島県の旧県社]] [[Category:鹿児島県の重要文化財]] [[Category:姶良市の歴史]] {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=鹿児島県}} [[Category:姶良市の建築物]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|300px]] |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|ja|姶良市|12=draft}}, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清|12=draft}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Osumi Province|ja|大隅国|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|ja|島津氏|12=draft}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|ja|島津義弘|12=draft}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|12=draft}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|12=draft}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|ja|和気清麻呂|12=draft}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Special Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]]

Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira, Kagoshima]], [[Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Chikakiyo|qq=}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|qq=}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihiro|Shimazu Yoshihiro]], who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|qq=}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kusu|qq=}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Special Natural Monuments of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[Category:姶良市の建築物]] [[Category:姶良市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]]

During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|qq=}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihiro|Shimazu Yoshihiro]], who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|qq=}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kusu|qq=}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[Category:Buildings in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:姶良市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}}

In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|qq=}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kusu|qq=}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] }} [[sn:Kamō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[sn:amō Hachiman Shrine (Aira)]] [[Category:Buildings in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aira]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}}

It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Chikakiyo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihiro|Shimazu Yoshihiro]], who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|qq=|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411552}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kusu|qq=}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] }} [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aira]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}} {{wikidata link|Q11621340}}

It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] }} [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aira]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}} {{wikidata link|Q11621340}}

Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} |location = {{ill|Aira|WD=Q407947}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira, Kagoshima]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihiro|Shimazu Yoshihiro]], who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411552}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kusu|qq=}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] }} [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aira]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}} {{wikidata link|Q11621340}}

It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihiro|Shimazu Yoshihiro]], who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411552}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kusu|qq=}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] }} [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aira]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}} {{wikidata link|Q11621340}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] }} [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aira]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}} {{wikidata link|Q11621340}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira]][[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11621340]] Wikidata name: Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11621340]] Wikidata name: Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kamō Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg |location = Kamōchō, Kamō, Aira City, Kagoshima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|31.765889|N|130.569694|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Emperor Ōjin
Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} |location = {{ill|Aira|WD=Q407947}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira, Kagoshima]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihiro|Shimazu Yoshihiro]], who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411552}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kusu|qq=}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] }} [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aira]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}} {{wikidata link|Q11621340}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} |location = {{ill|Aira|WD=Q407947}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira, Kagoshima]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihiro|Shimazu Yoshihiro]], who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411552}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kusu|qq=}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}} {{wikidata link|Q11621340}} [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Buildings in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aira]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} |location = {{ill|Aira|WD=Q407947}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira, Kagoshima]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]], Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first head of the [[:en:Gamō_clan|Gamō clan]], in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to [[:en:Ōsumi Province|Ōsumi Province]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the [[:en:Shimazu clan|Shimazu clan]] and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by [[:en:Shimazu Yoshihiro|Shimazu Yoshihiro]], who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411552}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kusu|qq=}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Wake no Kiyomaro]], is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}} {{wikidata link|Q11621340}} [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Buildings in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aira]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} |location = {{ill|Aira|WD=Q407947}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|en|Aira, Kagoshima|WD=Q407947|lt=Aira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first head of the {{ill|Gamō_clan|en|Gamō_clan|WD=Q2975251|lt=Gamō clan}}, in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|en|Shimazu Yoshihiro|WD=Q1156288|lt=Shimazu Yoshihiro}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411552}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kusu|qq=}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}} {{wikidata link|Q11621340}} [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Buildings in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aira]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} |location = {{ill|Aira|WD=Q407947|1=Aira, Kagoshima}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|en|Aira, Kagoshima|WD=Q407947|lt=Aira, Kagoshima|1=Aira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first head of the {{ill|Gamō_clan|en|Gamō_clan|WD=Q2975251|lt=Gamō clan|1=Gamō clan}}, in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|WD=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province|1=Ōsumi Province}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|WD=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan|1=Shimazu clan}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|en|Shimazu Yoshihiro|WD=Q1156288|lt=Shimazu Yoshihiro|1=Shimazu Yoshihiro}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11411552}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kusu|qq=}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}} {{wikidata link|Q11621340}} [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Buildings in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aira]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]
