Emperor Chūai
Empress Jingū |Rank = Former Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year = Hōan 4 (1123) |Main_Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |Alternate_names = Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū (former name) |Festivals = October 19 |Map = {{location map|Japan Kagoshima|label=Kamō Hachiman Shrine|coordinates={{coord|31|45|57.2|N|130|34|10.9|E}}|position=top|width=200}} |location = {{ill|Aira|qid=Q407947|1=Aira, Kagoshima}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Kamō Hachiman Shrine''' (蒲生八幡神社) is a [[Shinto]] shrine located in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|en|Aira, Kagoshima|qid=Q407947|lt=Aira, Kagoshima|1=Aira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|qid=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}, Japan. It was formerly known as '''Seihachiman Wakamiya Hachimangū'''. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the Hachiman Trinity: * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * Shisho Shrine (仁徳天皇, Uji Prince, Ure Princess, Kure Princess) * Takenouchi Shrine (武内宿禰) * Heavenly Shrine (天神七代) * National Shrine (地神五代) * Hayakaze Shrine (大人隼人) * Jūhasshu Shrine == History == Kamō Hachiman Shrine was founded by {{ill|Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Chikakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first head of the {{ill|Gamō_clan|en|Gamō_clan|qid=Q2975251|lt=Gamō clan|1=Gamō clan}}, in Hōan 4 (1123). Chikakiyo was connected to [[Usa Jingū]] through his mother, a member of the shrine's priestly family. The shrine was established after he moved to {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|qid=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province|1=Ōsumi Province}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period (Japan)|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period (Japan)|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}, the Kamō clan lost a fierce battle against the {{ill|Shimazu clan|en|Shimazu clan|qid=Q1061406|lt=Shimazu clan|1=Shimazu clan}} and was relocated in 1557 (Kōji 3). The shrine buildings were later reconstructed by {{ill|Shimazu Yoshihiro|en|Shimazu Yoshihiro|qid=Q1156288|lt=Shimazu Yoshihiro|1=Shimazu Yoshihiro}}, who also dedicated a torii gate and plaque in Genna 4 (1618). In 1985 (Shōwa 60), a typhoon severely damaged the shrine, and the current structure was rebuilt afterward. The name was officially changed to Kamō Hachiman Shrine the following year. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property === * Copper Mirror with Autumn Grass and Sparrows Design - A 13th-century copper mirror featuring intricate designs. It was designated a National Treasure under the {{ill|Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|ja|古社寺保存法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Act|1=Ancient Temples and Shrines Preservation Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11411552}} in 1918 and later categorized as an Important Cultural Property. The shrine holds 116 copper mirrors, the largest collection in Kagoshima Prefecture. === Special Natural Monument === * {{ill|Kamō no Kusu|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kusu|qq=}} - A giant camphor tree estimated to be 1,600 years old. It was designated as a Special Natural Monument in 1952. The tree, associated with {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|qid=Q7961005|lt=Wake no Kiyomaro|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}}, is said to have grown from a staff he planted. It is recognized as the largest tree in Japan. == Additional Information == * names of individuals involved in the shrine's reconstruction are inscribed on the back of the shrine's plaque. * A large Buddha statue was unearthed near the shrine during the relocation of a nearby cemetery in the early 20th century. == Notes == {{Reflist}} {{FootnoteHelp}} == External Links == * [http://www.kamou80000.com/index.html Official Website of Kamō Hachiman Shrine] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|version=101069439|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)]] [[ja:蒲生八幡神社]] {{Commons category|Kamouhachiman-jinja, Aira}} {{wikidata link|Q11621340}} [[Category:Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Aira, Kagoshima]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Aira]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kagoshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
